How I Stopped Writing Repetitive Code and Started Building Small Python Tools That Do the Work for Me
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Neuvalence on UnsplashAfter more than four years of working with Python professionally, I’ve noticed something interesting.
The developers who move fastest are not necessarily the ones who know the most algorithms, frameworks, or design patterns.
They’re usually the ones who automate everything.
Whenever I find myself repeating a task more than twice, I start asking a simple question:
“Can Python do this for me?”
Most of the time, the answer is yes.
In this article, I’ll share some of the automation techniques, patterns, and Python workflows that have saved me hundreds of hours over the years.
1. The Most Valuable Python Skill Isn’t Coding
When most people learn Python, they focus on syntax.
Variables.
Loops.
Functions.
Classes.
Those things matter.
